controls.title: Role-Based Access Control
controls.description: Fine-grained permission system with role mappings

evidence.snippet: * Authentication ok, but the user lacks the required permissions.
* `extra.issues` SHOULD contain the missing permissions for the caller to inspect.
* every route MUST carry a permission declaration produced by `rbacHandler(...)`
* permission declaration is automatically prepended with a `defaultDeny`
* permission is always served as 403 with a stable RFC 7807 error code,
*   `PERMISSION_DECLARED` symbol. The factory walks the registered handlers
* Handler attached automatically when no permission was declared on a route.
"This endpoint exists but no permission was registered. " +

evidence.snippet: * Whether WebAuthn / passkey passwordless authentication is enabled.

controls.title: Soft-Delete & Recovery

controls.title: Parameterized Queries (SQL Injection Prevention)
controls.description: All database queries use parameterized inputs

evidence.snippet: * No raw SQL — fully typed via DAL-pg query DSL.
const r = await pool.query<{ schema: string; table: string; col: string }>(
const existsR = await pool.query<{ oid: string }>(
const reg = await pool.query<{ oid: string | null }>(
* The display name fields are added by the query-builder when using the auditable join helpers.
* Value to pass to `node-pg` query parameters (or similar drivers).
* Build API URL with clientId and clientSecret as query parameters.
